108644.explanation: We believe that the quality of the person comes first.

108645.explanation: The Almelo council is the representative body of our municipality. The voter votes once every 4 years for a representative who makes these decisions on their behalf.

108648.explanation: Hospitality businesses should be able to choose their opening hours themselves but must also take responsibility for the environment.

108649.explanation: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should get more space from the municipality to come to a solution. We do this by fewer rules, not by financing. Financial benefits follow from the joint investment.

108650.explanation: Almelo must commit to more manufacturing industry and companies that fit the high-tech character of Twente.

108651.explanation: Our municipality contributes above average to the asylum task. We believe this can be reduced.

108655.explanation: People should be able to obtain permits themselves to organize local fireworks shows. The VVD keeps it humane.

108657.explanation: The noise standards at festivals are strict enough. People can take responsibility themselves by buying earplugs if they want extra protection.

108658.explanation: Entrepreneurship is also about accessibility. Financial support from the government is not directly logical.

108659.explanation: We support a high-quality, independent broadcaster in Almelo. These are currently 1Twente and Almelo1. They function in such a way that extra funding is not needed at this time.

108661.explanation: Not every child should be able to take music lessons through municipal subsidies. Only those children who cannot get music lessons without subsidies should have that opportunity.

108664.explanation: We want more law enforcement officers (BOA's) on the streets so people can walk safely and so the municipality can better enforce against nuisance.

108665.explanation: We want more camera surveillance on the streets so people can walk safely and so the municipality can better enforce against nuisance.

108667.explanation: BOA's are given more and more responsibilities and are increasingly exposed to unsafe situations. They may use their powers only under strict conditions.

108672.explanation: We focus on housing for young people.

108673.explanation: We believe that working is social. You contribute to society and reintegrate faster for your own well-being. Doing nothing is good for no one.

108674.explanation: The cancellation of taxes occurs because we want to prevent people from falling below the poverty line. We must prevent that.

108675.explanation: Income policy is the responsibility of the national government.

108677.explanation: When more people drive electric cars in Almelo, we must also install more charging stations.

108679.explanation: Introducing environmental zones is pestering drivers. We do not like that.

108680.explanation: The municipality must commit to good, functional care with caregivers who can embody that. If that can be diverse, that is fine — but ultimately health must be central in care.

108681.explanation: The municipality must choose good care at the best price.

108682.explanation: This is not a core task of the local government; we want to seek solutions together with local institutions.

108683.explanation: We believe that taxes should be as low as possible.

108690.explanation: Hof 88 has an incredibly important place in Almelo's cultural offerings.

108692.explanation: The municipality should contribute to the first sport for poor children, not to swimming lessons at school for everyone.

108693.explanation: Twents (the local dialect) is not a basic skill. Schools should focus on the essentials.

108696.explanation: With high-rise buildings, we make better use of existing space and build more for starters. We therefore believe we should build more upwards.

108697.explanation: Dividing houses into multiple rooms can contribute to the housing problem. If there is a demand for more rooms, that should be possible. This also helps to counteract issues related to labor migration.

108698.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality.

108699.explanation: Cheaper parking prices lead to more and longer visits to the center. This is good for Almelo's entrepreneurs and the enjoyment of the city center.

108701.explanation: That is bad for entrepreneurs and bad for the enjoyment of our residents. However, we do want to stimulate bicycle use.

108702.explanation: There are more important problems for us to focus on.

108704.explanation: We must build as much as possible.

108644.explanation: It would be good if it reflects society well, but we look at quality.

108645.explanation: We warmly welcome input from residents and there are several options besides a referendum for this.

108648.explanation: Yes, but intervention must be possible in case of nuisance.

108650.explanation: We see more opportunities for the manufacturing and defense industries.

108651.explanation: As long as there is a need, it is necessary, and if there is no longer a need, we can abolish it.

108655.explanation: Yes, but residents should also be able to set off fireworks.

108656.explanation: Preferably collected free at home rather than thrown somewhere in a ditch!

108657.explanation: The noise standards currently enforced are sufficient. It is about a good balance between enjoyment of living and also liveliness in the municipality.

108658.explanation: We are in favor of a fund aimed at improving accessibility for people with disabilities.

108659.explanation: The local media's main goal is to inform residents and must function well in this, if necessary with additional resources.

108661.explanation: This is good for the development of the children.

108664.explanation: All residents must feel safe in Almelo.

108665.explanation: In places where it is necessary

108667.explanation: Enforcement is part of the task of the community enforcement officers (boa's). They must have sufficient resources to actually enforce regulations.

108672.explanation: We provide good affordable housing for students.

108674.explanation: The tax burden is already quite high. They must be supported to make ends meet.

108675.explanation: People fall through the cracks. Work must pay off.

108677.explanation: This must not come at the expense of public parking spaces.

108680.explanation: That should not be part of providing care.

108681.explanation: Poor quality youth care for a lower price leads to higher demand for care. Everyone has the right to good care.

108682.explanation: Everyone must have the means to provide themselves with good personal hygiene without causing financial stress.

108690.explanation: Yes, this is part of the new plans.

108692.explanation: Learning to swim should not be optional. To give every child the same opportunities, this is best done through schools.

108693.explanation: Who finds the Twente language extremely important. Very beautiful man.

108696.explanation: Infill before expansion, even if this means some high residential towers. Only if these towers comply with the height vision.

108697.explanation: Room rental and shared housing are fine.

108698.explanation: We have that in Almelo and it must be preserved.

108699.explanation: To keep Almelo's city center lively, it must be as financially accessible as possible to come to Almelo.

108701.explanation: The center must remain accessible by car for people with mobility issues or for delivery to the shops.

108702.explanation: A tolerance policy is basically an odd construction. Legal hemp cultivation also ensures safer drugs, which leads to safer use.

108704.explanation: We want to build enough homes for young people, starters, and seniors.

108644.explanation: The CDA finds it important that men and women, people with disabilities, youth and seniors from every village and neighborhood are represented in our municipality, but we will not impose this by law.

108645.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. It is important to involve citizens in other ways on important topics.

108648.explanation: We are satisfied with how the municipality, hospitality sector, and residents have come to an agreement on hospitality opening hours.

108649.explanation: The CDA supports energy cooperatives and local residents who take initiatives to make the environment more sustainable. The municipality can support this.

108650.explanation: New businesses must add value to the local economy and employment opportunities for current residents. Distribution centers provide hardly any jobs that benefit the city.

108651.explanation: The CDA supports the dispersion law. This law ensures every municipality makes its proportional contribution to refugee reception.

108655.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the option for fireworks on New Year's Eve, even with a ban on consumer fireworks. This can be done by the municipality organizing its own fireworks display.

108656.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money. We pay waste disposal charges for that. The CDA wants to keep these charges as low as possible.

108657.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society. The municipality makes careful considerations when granting festival permits. Excessive noise nuisance must be prevented.

108658.explanation: The municipality has a task to make public spaces accessible. The CDA allocates funds for this. Keeping business buildings accessible is the responsibility of the businesses themselves.

108659.explanation: The regional broadcaster receives a contribution from the municipality. Nationally, a proposal is coming for extra funding for regional broadcasters from the national government. This is regulated in the Media Act.

108661.explanation: The CDA wants sports and culture to be accessible to all children.

108664.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More community enforcement officers (boa's) contribute to this. Almelo really needs to make progress in providing more enforcement officers.

108665.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Extra camera surveillance can detect crime more effectively and quickly, and greatly helps the police in tackling crime.

108667.explanation: The CDA in Almelo agrees with equipping boa’s with batons. Boa’s increasingly face difficult and sometimes unsafe situations. However, extra powers must come with clear frameworks, good training, and careful assessment. The safety of boa’s and residents comes first.

108672.explanation: Yes, but this must be done in cooperation with the housing associations.

108673.explanation: This already happens in our municipality and we will continue with it.

108674.explanation: The municipality must show its humane side: waiving municipal taxes is one of the most effective measures municipalities can take to support people with the lowest incomes.

108675.explanation: The CDA in Almelo wants to combat poverty but is against raising the income limit for assistance. A higher limit broadens schemes but does not make them more targeted. We choose tailored support so that aid reaches residents who truly need it.

108677.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

108679.explanation: The CDA questions whether this is truly a problem in Almelo. Measures have already been taken to exclude polluting vehicles from the city center.

108680.explanation: The CDA values choice options in healthcare. Sometimes the differences in rates are so large or the quality so low that it is undesirable for every care provider to be funded by the municipality.

108681.explanation: The CDA finds it important that the quality of care is good. Then price is not leading, but we do believe price has an important influence. The municipality must comply with national guidelines for reasonable tariffs.

108682.explanation: This can already be done through cooperation with the Poverty Fund, schools, and associations. Fortunately, we see the strength of society at work. Menstruation distribution points are a good example of this.

108683.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the burden on residents as low as possible.

108690.explanation: This does not fit within the plans for the cultural quarter and thus does not align with the cultural vision embraced by the CDA. The theater function of Hof 88 must of course have a place in the cultural quarter.

108692.explanation: Swimming skills of our children are important. The CDA is open to initiatives for school swimming.

108693.explanation: It is up to the primary schools themselves to give shape to this.

108696.explanation: High-rise buildings are necessary due to limited square meters and a high housing shortage.

108697.explanation: There is a need for room rental among part of society. Think, for example, of students or young people. There must be space for this. But only when living conditions are livable and good and it fits within current policy.

108698.explanation: The CDA finds this important in the context of meeting and combating loneliness. The CDA is pleased that this is already the case in almost all neighborhoods.

108699.explanation: The CDA is satisfied with the level of parking costs in the city center.

108701.explanation: The CDA is against a total ban on cars in the city center because that would make shops, hospitality, and services less accessible and could harm entrepreneurs. Additionally, city center residents often need a car for daily transport, caregiving, or small children.

108702.explanation: That attracts crime and the CDA is not in favor of that.

108704.explanation: The ambition of CDA Almelo is to have around 25,000 homes built by 2050. We want to achieve this by building together with neighboring municipalities Twenterand, Ommen, Daarlerveen, and Hardenberg to contribute to the national housing task.

108644.explanation: We stand for equal opportunities and diversity, but quality and content are decisive. Balance is important, without fixed quotas as a hard requirement.

108645.explanation: Residents should have more direct influence on decisions that affect them. Referenda strengthen local democracy and trust.

108648.explanation: More freedom creates liveliness; enforcement is needed in case of nuisance.

108649.explanation: Generating energy together lowers housing costs and increases residents’ control, especially for low incomes.

108650.explanation: Distribution centers deliver little good work, and labor migrants are often used to do the work. Distribution centers put great pressure on space, traffic, and housing.

108651.explanation: People who flee deserve shelter and perspective. Almelo remains in solidarity and complies with the distribution law.

108655.explanation: A central show is safer and prevents nuisance and injury. Possible alternative shows could also be organized with laser shows or drones.

108656.explanation: Free pickup prevents dumping and keeps neighborhoods clean. For people without a car to dispose of bulky waste, it would be a great solution.

108657.explanation: Events are part of a vibrant city; we seek balance with liveability.

108658.explanation: Accessibility is important, but not all costs can be borne by the municipality. The municipality must ensure that public spaces are accessible to everyone.

108659.explanation: Local media are important; extra support is possible but with clear agreements. Not only the official local broadcaster should be supported. Also, a local city broadcaster like Almelo 1 is of great value and may be financially supported.

108661.explanation: Cultural education increases opportunities and self-confidence and has a positive effect on the brain and learning performance.

108664.explanation: Safety requires a mix of enforcement and prevention.

108665.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a structural solution; prevention works better.

108667.explanation: BOAs are not police; de-escalation is paramount.

108672.explanation: If there is insufficient housing for students, this is certainly an option.

108673.explanation: Trust and support work better than coercion. Someone with social assistance benefits is not always able to perform work.

108674.explanation: The municipality should retain waivers because many residents with a low income, including workers, live around (or just above) the poverty line; with this support, you prevent people from falling into debt and limit greater social and financial problems in the long term.

108675.explanation: Increase the income limit because many workers currently fall just outside assistance while they have high fixed costs. With a higher limit, you prevent debts and stress and save later costs for debt counseling, care, and shelter.

108677.explanation: More charging points strengthen the charging infrastructure in the neighborhood so that the power grid and distribution of charging points can better grow with the number of EVs. This prevents peak pressure at a single pole and makes charging more reliable.

108679.explanation: Introduce an environmental zone to improve air quality and liveability in the center, but arrange exceptions/exemptions for residents. Not everyone can afford a newer car, and people must be able to get to work; prevent the measure from mainly affecting low incomes.

108680.explanation: Everyone deserves respectful, appropriate care, but “always being able to choose” is not feasible and can lead to exclusion or inequality. More important is that every care provider works culture-sensitively and takes preferences into account, without compromising quality.

108681.explanation: Quality must come first in youth care. Too much focus on the lowest price more often leads to changes and less effective help, causing problems to worsen and costs to rise later.

108682.explanation: Free tampons and sanitary pads prevent period poverty, so that no one has to miss school, work, or sports. It is a small investment that increases health, dignity, and equal opportunities.

108683.explanation: Lowering the property tax sounds nice, but the budget gap then returns as less maintenance and public amenities or higher other levies. It is better to provide targeted support for those who need it, instead of a general discount for everyone.

108690.explanation: Renew Hof 88, possibly at a different location, so Almelo maintains a strong cultural offering. Culture enriches the city, brings people together, and makes Almelo more attractive to live in and visit, with positive effects for the city center and local economy.

108692.explanation: Allocate funds for school swimming lessons, because swimming skills are a basic safety requirement in a country full of water. It reduces drowning risks, decreases inequality (not every family can afford lessons) and gives children a skill they will benefit from their whole lives.

108693.explanation: Give attention to the Twents dialect in all primary schools, so children learn the regional language and local culture. This strengthens identity and connection, keeps Twents alive and can stimulate language awareness, without having to come at the expense of Dutch.

108696.explanation: More high residential towers help solve the housing shortage faster. Building upwards uses less land, prevents unnecessary damage to nature and rural areas, and efficiently provides many homes on a limited number of square meters, preferably close to amenities and public transport.

108697.explanation: Room rental causes pressure on livability in the neighborhood.

108698.explanation: A community center in every neighborhood strengthens social interaction and cohesion, helps combat loneliness, and provides a place for youth, elderly, and volunteers. It makes help and activities easily accessible nearby and prevents small problems from growing bigger.

108699.explanation: Paid parking in the city center is already reasonably affordable. Keep the rate stable and rather arrange smart short-term parking and targeted solutions for residents and entrepreneurs.

108701.explanation: Make the city center car-free for more safety, cleaner air, calm, and space for shops and terraces. However, keep access for residents and necessary destination traffic, for example with exemptions, time windows for loading and unloading, and good alternatives such as peripheral parking.

108702.explanation: Participating in the cannabis experiment closes the coffee shop chain (legal cultivation, controlled quality), reduces crime and unsafe products, and provides data for better policy. Almelo is currently not one of the 10 municipalities, so can only participate if the national government opens additional spots.

108704.explanation: The number should not go down, because there is a housing shortage and demand is growing. Building sufficiently can contribute to affordable housing and create space for starters, families, and the elderly.

108644.explanation: It is extremely important that women are represented in local government, preferably in equal proportion.

108645.explanation: The introduction of municipal referendums and citizen assemblies belongs to a modern democracy according to D66. A prerequisite is that the referendum topic is clearly defined and the consequences of the different outcomes are clear to everyone in advance.

108648.explanation: We give entrepreneurs more freedom for alternative opening hours. Opening hours for hospitality businesses are set in a regulation, with the possibility of customized arrangements when nuisance is carefully prevented and addressed.

108649.explanation: Revenue from sustainable energy must remain within Almelo via local cooperatives. The municipality should support these initiatives by working with the grid operator to ensure connection to the electricity grid. We also encourage this financially, for example by not charging fees for this.

108650.explanation: We choose to maintain and strengthen existing and already planned business parks. Further expansion is not needed now; the focus is on quality, sustainability, and efficient use of the space that already exists. At XL Businesspark 2, agreements have been made about the types of businesses that fit.

108651.explanation: If the situation requires, Almelo takes responsibility to shelter refugees. D66 stands for good, humane accommodation, such as in the renewed asylum seekers center (AZC).

108655.explanation: It is a nice gesture to organize a show with fireworks or drones now that the national fireworks ban has come into effect.

108656.explanation: The principle is that the polluter pays. Residents should be able to easily dispose of their well-separated waste to keep costs low.

108657.explanation: Noise standards are mainly determined by data and research and are not so much political. However, consideration must be given to how often an area is burdened by events and whether music can be softer after a certain time.

108658.explanation: It is up to entrepreneurs to organize their business to accommodate everyone. The municipality wants to engage and facilitate input from people with disabilities to bring this to attention.

108659.explanation: The municipal council currently provides a fixed amount to the local broadcaster. When new legislation comes into effect, this money will come directly from the national government. Until then, we will not change the amount.

108661.explanation: D66 Almelo chooses lifelong learning, where everyone has the opportunity to develop. Providing good education means ensuring equal opportunities, personal growth, and an open society. Music lessons are part of this.

108664.explanation: The deployment of enforcement officers (BOA’s) is always supplementary to the police. BOA’s add value and we want to expand capacity, among other things for more safety around hospitality venues. D66 wants to prevent police from shifting tasks to BOA’s due to capacity shortages.

108665.explanation: D66 only supports temporary and targeted cameras in entertainment areas, such as Amaliaplein, with maximum privacy safeguards. We only want preventive camera surveillance used if strict conditions are attached.

108667.explanation: This is not decided by the municipality but by the public prosecutor.

108672.explanation: Offer temporary living spaces to provide shelter for people in difficult situations. These spaces come with conditions and restrictions such as a maximum rental period.

108673.explanation: For those who cannot work, the municipality helps them participate in society, for example through volunteering. This is not possible for everyone.

108674.explanation: When local taxes increase, the burden is fairly distributed and vulnerable residents are spared. Poverty policy focuses on addressing causes and recognizing hidden poverty, with special attention for families and self-employed individuals.

108675.explanation: Poverty policy focuses on addressing causes and recognizing hidden poverty, with special attention for families and self-employed individuals. Raising the income threshold can help in recognition.

108677.explanation: We stimulate the placement of public charging stations where there is demand. Where residents already have their own charging point, placement by the municipality is not necessary. Charging stations and parking spaces are combined with solar panels where possible.

108679.explanation: Our neighborhoods and business parks must become climate neutral. Through subsidies, we help residents and businesses make sustainable improvements. We encourage entrepreneurs in the city center to be supplied as sustainably as possible. An environmental zone is possible but not yet considered.

108680.explanation: You have the freedom to make choices, including about care. Care providers also have the freedom to be who they are. If a belief or conviction has no consequence for the provision of care, it may not be used as a reason to refuse care.

108681.explanation: When there are shortages in youth care, D66 prioritizes care for children and young people. Almelo is reforming youth care to intervene earlier and invest in prevention. This leads to better, faster help with less waiting time and lower costs.

108682.explanation: Menstrual products must be accessible to every woman. D66 believes Almelo should facilitate this within the municipal workplace. How to do this outside the city hall is important to investigate.

108683.explanation: To maintain local amenities such as care, culture, and sports, a moderate and fair increase of the property tax can be considered, with the burden fairly distributed and vulnerable residents spared.

108690.explanation: We will continue to subsidize Hof88 to guarantee theater offerings for Almelo. Hof88 and the art hall will move to a cultural quarter in the city center.

108692.explanation: Swimming lessons are accessible to all children.

108693.explanation: D66 Almelo wants an education vision in which important choices can be made. We choose more sports and exercise and the German language. Twents language and culture can also be included in this vision.

108696.explanation: Almelo must embrace smart housing concepts for single-person households, both young and old. We will also build upwards in parts of the city to make this possible.

108697.explanation: D66 wants to make room rentals possible for, among others, young people. The quality of this is assessed beforehand and later strictly monitored by the municipality.

108698.explanation: Each neighborhood has a meeting place with a social function. This does not have to be a specially designed building for that purpose, but can also be housed at a local association. The municipality owns neighborhood centers so that volunteers can be involved in activities.

108699.explanation: Cheaper or free parking for visitors means costs elsewhere for residents. We simplify the parking policy in the city center. Paid parking applies within the parking ring. Residents must be able to park, but visitors are mainly directed to parking garages.

108701.explanation: D66 supports reducing car traffic in the city center as much as possible. The streets in the center are primarily for pedestrians and cyclists. Residents must be able to park, but visitors are mainly directed to parking garages.

108702.explanation: The current setup of the experiment for legal cultivation is unfortunately doomed to fail. D66 supports the legalization of cannabis but does not see much value in small-scale trials on a scale like Almelo, especially not without cooperation from Hengelo and Enschede.

108704.explanation: The numbers we have agreed upon are needed to take the first steps against the housing crisis. D66 emphasizes that we are a central municipality in Twente. Almelo, Aadorp and Bornerbroek will receive a balanced housing stock.

108644.explanation: Both women and men can fulfill these roles. However, the qualities of the candidates should be the primary consideration.

108645.explanation: The ChristenUnie is very hesitant about a corrective referendum. It is not a good instrument for making important and usually complex decisions. An open dialogue and trust lead to better decisions.

108648.explanation: We advocate for extended closing times so visitors can leave gradually, but within the framework set by the municipal council.

108649.explanation: We support local initiatives in the field of sustainable energy. Cooperatives like Almelo Energie, in which residents join forces, are good partners for the municipality. For healthy operations, no subsidy from the municipality is needed.

108650.explanation: XL Businesspark 1 and 2 are not intended for logistics but for manufacturing industry. This provides more and higher quality employment than large distribution centers. This employment is needed for Almelo.

108651.explanation: Every municipality has a responsibility in housing asylum seekers to ensure national distribution, and we contribute generously to this.

108655.explanation: If the fireworks ban actually comes into effect in 2026, we advocate for a large central event to usher in the new year. A large professional fireworks show is part of that.

108656.explanation: The municipality is obliged to provide a good waste collection service. Not everyone is able to bring bulky waste themselves to the Turfkade. The current arrangement is sufficient and can remain free.

108657.explanation: The current noise standards for festivals are satisfactory for almost everyone—both local residents and festival attendees. There is no need to make the standards stricter.

108658.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for the accessibility of their buildings.

108659.explanation: The municipality financially supports one local broadcaster, which is 1Twente. Additionally, the municipality promotes collaboration between 1Twente and Almelo1.

108661.explanation: Music lessons are currently part of education in primary schools. The Youth Fund can possibly contribute financially for extra music lessons, for example at the music school.

108664.explanation: The presence and visibility of enforcement officers increase physical and social safety.

108665.explanation: Technical measures such as cameras are not an end in themselves but a means and are only deployed where necessary and effective for combating crime or increasing safety and the feeling of safety. Possible privacy infringements are carefully weighed.

108667.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with the police and not with a community enforcement officer (BOA). Exceptions can be made in specific cases.

108672.explanation: There is no shortage of student housing in Almelo.

108673.explanation: Obligations may be attached to social assistance. But these obligations are not an end in themselves. They must help people find a place in society.

108674.explanation: Almelo applies a social remission and collection policy when collecting municipal taxes and levies to prevent problematic debts.

108675.explanation: People who get into financial trouble must be helped quickly and effectively. That means simple and accessible debt counseling.

108677.explanation: The number of charging stations will be expanded to enable the growth of electric cars.

108679.explanation: The ChristenUnie stands for good accessibility in Almelo and encourages choosing the most sustainable option. Therefore, we want to investigate the possible introduction of an environmental zone for polluting scooters, cars, and trucks. We take into account experiences in other municipalities.

108680.explanation: Good cooperation between informal and formal care is necessary. For certain help requests from residents, professional involvement is appropriate. Informal help cannot replace all care. Care that fits someone's beliefs contributes to well-being and also to cooperation with informal caregivers.

108681.explanation: Organizations for youth assistance are selected based on good price-quality ratio. The price is important but subordinate to the quality of care.

108682.explanation: Period poverty is a fact. In exceptional situations, free distribution is possible at special pickup points.

108683.explanation: Municipalities receive structurally too little from the national government, but expenditures and revenues must be balanced. The first approach is to be cost-conscious. We handle other people's money carefully. At the same time, we must do what is necessary! Therefore, there is no taboo on increasing property tax (OZB).

108690.explanation: Renovation yes, relocation no (preserve Chapel). The concept of theater and film house is good.

108692.explanation: ChristenUnie finds it important that children are skilled swimmers. Parents can receive (full) reimbursement for swimming lessons through the Youth Fund. Due to the high costs, funding school swimming is a less good solution.

108693.explanation: Attention to the regional language is an important value for social cohesion. However, we should not burden primary education with this. It is more efficient to convey this message to youth and young adults through other channels.

108696.explanation: There is a need for smaller and affordable housing. This can be well achieved by constructing high-rise buildings in the center of Almelo.

108697.explanation: This has a huge impact on already socially weaker neighborhoods. Slumlords buy up houses and rent out rooms to labor migrants. This causes disruption.

108698.explanation: We support neighborhood centers where people come together for a listening ear. Association life is important for connection in society, this helps establish meaningful social contacts.

108699.explanation: Parking in the center of Almelo is not expensive. By asking a small contribution, it is encouraged to come to the city center by other means of transport where possible and when possible.

108701.explanation: ChristenUnie supports zero emissions. The shopping area in Almelo is free from motorized traffic. However, the center must remain accessible to residents. A car-free city center in Almelo is practically difficult to implement.

108702.explanation: The ChristenUnie is against the cultivation of cannabis by municipalities.

108704.explanation: In Almelo, we want to build according to demand. We invest in sufficient and affordable sustainable housing. The current housing supply is too low, so larger numbers of homes need to be built.

108644.explanation: Positions in the municipal executive should be filled based on quality and suitability, not based on gender.

108645.explanation: Referendums give residents direct influence on important decisions and strengthen democracy. Politics must listen to the will of the people, not just make decisions behind closed doors.

108648.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves when they are open. Less government interference creates space for entrepreneurship and allows businesses to better respond to their customers.

108649.explanation: The municipality should focus on its core tasks and not on financing energy projects. Those who want to generate energy together can do so without using other people's tax money.

108650.explanation: Large distribution centers create few jobs but cause a lot of freight traffic and damage to the landscape. They take up a lot of space without the local community truly benefiting, so the municipality must counter these developments.

108651.explanation: Asylum reception has a negative impact on safety and the sense of security in neighborhoods. Residents experience more unrest and incidents, while enforcement and police are already heavily burdened.

108655.explanation: A municipal fireworks show can be organized but must not replace the traditional New Year's Eve celebrations. The objective should be to return to how New Year's was always celebrated.

108656.explanation: It is a nice idea, but it comes with many costs for the municipality. Therefore, it is reasonable that residents remain responsible for disposing of their bulky waste.

108657.explanation: Events are part of a vibrant city and should not be stifled by increasingly strict rules; enforcement is preferable to imposing new restrictions that affect organizers and entrepreneurs.

108659.explanation: The local broadcaster must remain independent and self-sufficient. Additional municipal funding means higher costs for taxpayers and increases the political dependence of the media.

108661.explanation: Music lessons are valuable but not a core task of the municipality. However, there should be room for targeted support for families who cannot afford this themselves, so talented children are not excluded.

108664.explanation: More enforcement officers ensure visible presence and increased safety in neighborhoods. At the same time, deploying actual police officers must be a priority, as they have more powers and authority to effectively tackle crime and nuisance.

108665.explanation: More camera surveillance helps prevent and solve crime faster. Visible cameras increase the sense of security and support police and enforcement officers in maintaining order.

108667.explanation: Community enforcement officers working on safety in Almelo are increasingly confronted with aggression and threats. A baton enhances their own safety and enables them to respond better to dangerous situations until the police arrive.

108672.explanation: Temporary student housing, such as container homes, is not a structural solution for the housing shortage in Almelo. Municipal funds are better spent on sustainable homes for residents who want to live and work here long-term.

108673.explanation: A social assistance benefit should not be a dead end. By having people contribute through socially valuable work, the connection to work and responsibility remains, and system abuse is prevented.

108674.explanation: The municipality should support low-income residents, but permanently waiving taxes is not always the right solution. Targeted and temporary support is fairer and prevents people from becoming dependent on arrangements for a long time.

108675.explanation: Help should reach those who really need it. Raising the income threshold increases dependency on municipal support and costs, while customized and temporary assistance is more effective than structurally expanding schemes.

108677.explanation: Designating charging spots favors a small group who can afford an electric car. This comes at the expense of parking spaces for residents who cannot afford these cars.

108679.explanation: Argument: An environmental zone in Almelo mainly affects ordinary residents and entrepreneurs who depend on their car. Banning older vehicles makes the city center less accessible and favors those who can afford a new car.

108680.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is important but cannot always be guaranteed. Quality and availability of care must come first; personal preferences should not lead to extra pressure, higher costs, or unequal access to care.

108681.explanation: Price is important to keep healthcare affordable but should not be decisive. In youth care, quality, reliability, and effectiveness must always weigh heavier than the lowest costs alone.

108682.explanation: Distributing menstrual products free of charge to everyone goes too far. The municipality can support residents who truly cannot afford them, so help goes where it is really needed.

108683.explanation: A lower property tax gives residents and entrepreneurs more financial room. The municipality should critically assess its expenditures instead of constantly shifting the burden onto homeowners.

108690.explanation: Theater Hof 88 has value for the city, but renewal is not a core task of the municipality. Large-scale investments must not come at the expense of more important municipal tasks.

108692.explanation: School swimming contributes to children's safety and prevents accidents. It is a practical investment that ensures every child learns to swim, regardless of their home situation.

108693.explanation: The Twents dialect is part of local culture and identity. By paying attention to it in primary schools, traditions and language are preserved without negatively affecting regular education.

108696.explanation: Young people in Almelo struggle to find affordable housing. Additional apartment buildings can help with this.

108697.explanation: Splitting homes into separate rooms does not solve the housing shortage structurally. It mainly puts pressure on neighborhoods and livability, whereas the municipality should promote renting out entire homes to maintain neighborhood cohesion and better prevent problems.

108698.explanation: Community centers can be valuable, but a center in every neighborhood is not necessary and costly. The municipality should assess where there is real demand and better utilize existing facilities instead of creating new centers everywhere.

108699.explanation: Cheaper paid parking in Almelo's city center keeps the center accessible and attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs.

108701.explanation: A car-free city center in Almelo makes the center less accessible for residents and entrepreneurs. Cars are necessary for many people; completely banning them harms liveliness and the local economy.

108702.explanation: Registering as a pilot municipality for legal cannabis cultivation carries risks for safety and enforcement and does not align with Almelo's priorities.

108704.explanation: Sufficient housing must be built to address the housing shortage, but primarily within the city of Almelo. The surrounding countryside should be spared as much as possible to preserve landscape and living environment, and growth should happen responsibly.

108644.explanation: No, a municipal executive does not need to have an equal distribution between men and women; knowledge and experience remain more important.

108645.explanation: Yes, the Party Vrij Almelo advocates for referendums because we believe the citizen should be central.

108648.explanation: Yes, cafes and restaurants may basically determine their own opening hours but must comply with national and especially local regulations.

108649.explanation: Yes, residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

108650.explanation: The Free Almelo Party is strongly opposed to the development of large new distribution centers and business parks. The party wants to put a stop to further logistical expansion to protect livability and the landscape.

108651.explanation: Free Almelo Party is against more asylum accommodation and wants to close the door for new locations because we believe Almelo is already doing enough and the policy is flawed.

108655.explanation: The municipality of Almelo should switch to a central fireworks show instead of individual fireworks.

108656.explanation: Free home pickup is rare; you should expect to pay for the service unless it concerns a specific free service.

108657.explanation: Noise nuisance leads to stress, high blood pressure, sleep disorders, and even cardiovascular diseases.

108658.explanation: Yes, the municipality must financially assist entrepreneurs in making their buildings accessible to people with disabilities, supported by legislation such as the Equal Treatment Act on the grounds of disability or chronic illness.

108659.explanation: Free Almelo Party supports the local broadcaster and has previously requested extra financial support (such as a one-time €50,000) and attention for the funding.

108661.explanation: Subsidies ensure that every child, regardless of their parents' financial situation, comes into contact with music.

108664.explanation: The Free Almelo Party advocates for better accessibility and visibility of neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers, with the 'know and be recognized' principle central to detecting early signals from residents.

108665.explanation: No, more active enforcement and visibility of enforcement officers and police, more prevention.

108667.explanation: Enforcement officers should be equipped with batons to increase their safety and to be able to de-escalate in cases of increasing violence and aggression on the streets.

108672.explanation: No, unlike places such as Enschede (University, Saxion), Almelo is not a typical student city with high demand for student housing.

108673.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits must provide a 'counter-performance', which is socially useful work such as volunteering; however, it is important that people with participation jobs have a clear prospect of a regular job.

108674.explanation: No, the waiver of municipal taxes for low incomes is part of the overall purchasing power picture. However, there should be more control over improper use of provisions.

108675.explanation: The Free Almelo Party (PVA) advocates raising income thresholds for combating poverty because current standards, such as those for the Individual Income Supplement (often 101% of the social assistance norm), are too low.

108677.explanation: The Free Almelo Party advocates for more parking spaces as charging points and links this to stimulating a busier center through free parking, although the focus on EV charging stations is more of a general parking strategy.

108679.explanation: The Free Almelo Party (PVA) is generally critical of (expensive) environmental measures and advocates practical solutions that do not overly burden the citizen.

108680.explanation: The ability to choose a caregiver who fits one's faith, culture, or beliefs is a fundamental right and a goal within Dutch healthcare.

108681.explanation: Free Almelo Party wants the focus to be on reducing intake into youth care through prevention and targeted deployment, rather than only selecting on the lowest price, to ensure quality and control rising costs.

108682.explanation: Yes, the poverty fund facilitates *free* menstrual products, so-called MUPs. About 1 in 8-10 women cannot afford them. Supported as a pilot project.

108683.explanation: The Free Almelo Party has spoken out against extra increases beyond the regular indexation because they believe residents already pay enough.

108690.explanation: We choose a new City Theater where Hof 88 can optimally continue its smaller-scale activities in a modern building.

108692.explanation: The Free Almelo Party advocates that all children in Almelo have access to swimming skills and asks about the possibilities to realize and finance this.

108693.explanation: The Free Almelo Party supports the active group of people and organizations that strive to keep the Twente dialect alive, supported by official recognition as a regional language, which is essential for the cultural richness of the region.

108696.explanation: Free Almelo Party opposes excessive high-rise buildings in Almelo and strives for a better balanced housing policy that takes citizens and city quality into account.

108697.explanation: Free Almelo could embrace this initiative as it fits limiting 'slumlords' and preserving livability, something already initiated in Almelo with measures like buy-back protection.

108698.explanation: The Free Almelo Party (PVA) actively works to preserve and strengthen community centers in neighborhoods. The party sees these locations as essential for livability and combating loneliness, especially among the elderly.

108699.explanation: Free Almelo Party favors free parking, and although the municipality has partially introduced free parking, this is a temporary action from the council, aligning with the broader goal of cheaper parking policy.

108701.explanation: The Free Almelo Party advocates for a car-free city center, provided that sufficient parking spaces are created in the immediate vicinity of the center so that the inner city can be reached on foot within about 5 minutes.

108702.explanation: Free Almelo Party is against legalization initiatives: PVA focuses on enforcement and the 'power of the neighborhood' to combat undermining crime such as cannabis cultivation.

108704.explanation: The Free Almelo Party is not against housing construction but wants realistic growth while preserving the village character; and is critical of the large-scale plan for 14,000 homes as it puts pressure on Almelo’s livability, greenery, and infrastructure.

108644.explanation: It is good to strive for a fair distribution without making it an end in itself to have an equal number of men and women in these positions.

108645.explanation: With a binding corrective referendum, we gain control over matters that concern us all. The population gets the right to call out politics when decisions are made against the will of the people. This way, we keep democracy representative.

108648.explanation: It is the municipality’s task to oversee the opening hours of hospitality venues. This is not just about economic interests (entrepreneurs) but also about the residential and living environment and thus the interests of local residents. We do not think letting go of this is a good idea.

108649.explanation: For Almelo residents who want to make their homes more sustainable, both national and municipal subsidies are available. What the SP finds important is that these subsidies reach people who otherwise cannot afford the measures (property value ceiling).

108650.explanation: Almelo has plans for XL Business Park 2. As SP, we are not against economic growth and employment, but economic growth must demonstrably benefit the working class. Often, mainly labor migrants work in distribution centers, with additional nuisance in neighborhoods.

108651.explanation: We help and humanely accommodate people fleeing war and violence. Almelo takes its fair share in this.

108655.explanation: Now that the national fireworks ban is in effect, it is good to offer people an alternative. A municipal fireworks show can be a nice event.

108656.explanation: We agree with this, but it is already possible for residents of Almelo to have bulky waste (which often does not fit or is not allowed in home containers) collected free of charge by Twente Milieu. You need an environmental pass with an annual disposal quota of 300 kilos.

108657.explanation: If there is a reason to change this, then it should be possible.

108658.explanation: Entrepreneurs must take care of that themselves. The municipality is responsible for public buildings, and there is still much room for improvement there as well.

108659.explanation: 1Twente has received enough funding for what it has achieved so far.

108661.explanation: This should be part of the education curriculum.

108664.explanation: This costs a lot of money. If more effort is needed in the area of safety, it is primarily the responsibility of the police force.

108665.explanation: Camera surveillance is a technology that can make the work of officers more effective, but it should not be just a symbolic measure replacing officers. Camera surveillance does not prevent crimes but can assist in investigations.

108667.explanation: The monopoly on violence belongs to the police and should not be extended to special enforcement officers (BOAs).

108672.explanation: We wonder if this is an issue in Almelo.

108673.explanation: Volunteering is voluntary and regular work must be rewarded with a salary. The SP has always opposed a mandatory 'reciprocal contribution' in social assistance. People on social assistance must be guaranteed work and a suitable income. We do not allow working without pay.

108674.explanation: Completely disagree. Everyone should oppose this because stopping this only drives people further into debt, which is not in anyone's interest.

108675.explanation: Everyone should oppose this because stopping this only drives people further into debt, which is not in anyone's interest.

108677.explanation: We have not received signals that the municipality needs to designate more charging points for electric vehicles.

108679.explanation: The goal of the environmental zone – cleaner air – is good, but the question is whether the right means are being used. We prefer that as few cars as possible enter the city center at all. We achieve this with sufficient parking options at the city edge and good public transport.

108680.explanation: Unfortunately, that is not always possible. There is already a shortage of staff in healthcare.

108681.explanation: This method of selection contributes to a race to the bottom in youth care quality.

108682.explanation: This is already provided through the food bank. Ideally, social assistance or minimum wage should allow one to purchase this themselves. There is a certain humiliation attached to having to distribute this.

108683.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is one of the ways the municipality can generate income. We support a tax burden based on ability to pay, where those with the broadest shoulders carry the heaviest load. Someone with a spacious home can pay more than someone with a small house.

108690.explanation: The current building has an energy label that is almost off the scale. If possible, we should improve that, but not at all costs.

108692.explanation: The national government must finance this. This is possible if school swimming is made part of the school program.

108693.explanation: Twents is an important part of our culture and must be preserved and promoted.

108696.explanation: Due to poor political choices, too few houses have been built in recent years. The SP is not against apartment buildings.

108697.explanation: Homes are for living in, not for profit. Fortunately, we have a purchase protection for existing homes and an occupancy obligation for new homes in the municipality of Almelo. Room rentals are also subject to strict rules and only allowed with a permit.

108698.explanation: You can unite people through associations; these bring people together and help us bridge differences. Every neighborhood in our city and every village in our municipality deserves a community center.

108699.explanation: We want to make Almelo’s center low-traffic. Incoming car traffic will be directed to parking garages. Visitors should not park in the center itself.

108701.explanation: We want to make Almelo’s center low-traffic. Incoming car traffic will be directed to parking garages. Visitors should not park in the center itself.

108702.explanation: We prefer legal cannabis cultivation done in a legal, regulated way rather than unsafe and criminal ways.

108704.explanation: Due to poor political choices, too few houses have been built in recent years. We must continue to adhere to the building plans.

108644.explanation: As an aim, that is fine, but if there are more women than men in the college, that is perfectly acceptable as well! This is a topic for after the elections; it is up to the parties forming the coalition.

108645.explanation: It is especially important that residents can participate much more than now in thinking and discussing decision-making about important topics beforehand. The municipality must take residents much more seriously in this respect: stimulate more participation with the council and, even more importantly, actually do something with it.

108648.explanation: The municipality sets the frameworks within which hospitality businesses are free to determine their opening hours within those frameworks. It is important that the municipality continues to set these frameworks, especially regarding closing times.

108649.explanation: It is good to stimulate this kind of initiative, but government subsidies should not be automatic. It is important to see if there is a business model behind an initiative and whether there are other forms of financing present or possible.

108650.explanation: Almelo already has too many large-scale distribution centers, companies that have a negative impact due to their significant intrusion into residential and living environments and the fact that they hardly create employment for residents of Almelo and surroundings.

108651.explanation: This is an improper and inhumane stance. The municipality of Almelo must fulfill its human duty and provide shelter to people fleeing persecution and war violence.

108655.explanation: A central fireworks show has become an option due to the nationwide fireworks ban. Whether the municipality will organize this depends on the residents' interest. They should have a choice. A survey must be organized for this, also clarifying how much such a show would cost.

108656.explanation: This pickup can be made by appointment. This service is important for people who cannot transport their bulky waste to the disposal point. In addition, Almelo Centraal wants to encourage people who properly bring bulky waste by rewarding them.

108657.explanation: Stricter noise standards help prevent nuisance and can also reduce the need for enforcement during festivals and parties.

108658.explanation: Creating accessibility is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality can encourage entrepreneurs with information, advice, and the like.

108659.explanation: Omroep Almelo was unjustly sidelined by a majority of the council in the past term. Almelo1, a streamlined continuation of Omroep Almelo, now operates without subsidy but definitely deserves financial support.

108661.explanation: In principle, every child should have access to music lessons. If the parents' income is insufficient, the municipality should subsidize the music lessons. (By the way, the Youth Culture Fund already exists for this, which will certainly cover part of the demand.)

108664.explanation: Preferably more prevention than more enforcement. Moreover, insecurity is often more a feeling than an objective truth. Reducing feelings of insecurity and strengthening the sense of safety must be done in other ways, including good communication.

108665.explanation: There is sufficient camera surveillance in Almelo. Moreover, camera surveillance offers a form of false security. However, if incidents repeatedly occur at a certain place, temporary (mobile) camera surveillance can be deployed.

108667.explanation: In principle, Almelo Centraal is against this. However, if in the future the demand for a baton by the BOAs themselves arises (in the interest of their own safety), this should be open for discussion. For order maintenance, this concerns a short baton.

108672.explanation: If there is a need for this from the Twente student cities (Enschede, Hengelo), it can certainly be discussed. As a category of residents, students contribute to a more diverse, attractive city.

108673.explanation: Almelo Centraal wants the municipality to encourage residents to be active in society. The vast majority of people are on welfare due to the circumstances in which they (unwillingly) find themselves. A mandatory reciprocal obligation is not appropriate. Encouragement achieves the most.

108674.explanation: Debt relief is an important part of Almelo's poverty policy, which is becoming increasingly sparse.

108675.explanation: Raising the income threshold means that more people qualify for assistance. Almelo Centraal supports this. The income limit for Almelo's schemes is often too low.

108677.explanation: The municipality must keep up with the increasing demand for electric charging stations, but given the scarcity of public space, it should not create a surplus either.

108679.explanation: The center of Almelo is too small an area for this and also has important thoroughfares. Moreover, when it comes to health and quality of the living environment, it is odd to distinguish between the center and other parts of the city.

108680.explanation: The municipality must ensure as wide a range as possible, where the quality of care (and relative to that quality the costs) is the criterion. As a local government, the municipality adopts a neutral stance regarding religion.

108681.explanation: The quality of assistance must always come first. In all forms of assistance and especially in youth care.

108682.explanation: This is not a municipal task. It is up to the municipality to alleviate the financial poverty of its residents as much as possible, but not through this kind of micro-measures.

108683.explanation: Financially, this is (unfortunately) not feasible for Almelo; revenues from property tax are needed to finance municipal provisions.

108690.explanation: Yes: at a new location. (This will create space for housing at Hofkampstraat.) The condition is that it remains a small-scale theater. Almelo already has a large-scale theater: the City Theater.

108692.explanation: Namely for those families for whom school swimming is too expensive. For the safety of children (and also later when they are adults), it is important to be able to swim.

108693.explanation: If there are schools and/or parents who need attention for the Twents dialect, they are of course free to do so themselves, but they must arrange this themselves.

108696.explanation: It is important that the houses are of good quality, that they meet the wishes of current home seekers and are future-proof and, if possible, suitable for all life stages. Also important: the quality of the living environment and sufficient supply for people from lower income groups.

108697.explanation: The municipality should look per neighborhood/district at what is possible and set quotas. It is important that there is no nuisance to neighbors and also that the living conditions for room renters themselves are good, with normal rents, decent sizes, and thus without exploitation.

108698.explanation: Neighborhood and village community centers are part of the basic amenities in a city. The municipality can stimulate these in various ways, preferably in combination with other provisions (e.g., sports, care, education). Neighborhood centers do not need to be literally in every neighborhood, but where there is a need.

108699.explanation: Parking should not be a cash cow for the municipality. It's about balancing discouraging car traffic to the city center and keeping the center attractive and accessible for every budget. We choose 'neither' here because parking certainly should not become more expensive.

108701.explanation: It is wise to look at places where cars can be banned in favor of cyclists, pedestrians, local residents, and visitors, but making the entire city center car-free is a bad idea.

108702.explanation: It is important that there are pilot municipalities, but experiences elsewhere have shown what an enormous burden this places on the administrative apparatus. Almelo is not equipped for this.

108704.explanation: Ambitions are never sacred in themselves. Plans should be developed step by step; the municipality should not sell land unnecessarily to developers. Careful handling of scarce space and safeguarding the quality of living and the living environment should always come first.

108644.explanation: At least equal, because equal representation is important, but for Leefbaar Almelo it may also be more women than men.

108645.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes that in the elections, voters already express their opinion based on their vote on their representative, and a referendum rarely yields better results than a well-considered council decision.

108648.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo assumes that (hospitality) entrepreneurs who receive a permit are capable of determining the best opening hours for their business themselves.

108649.explanation: Where needed, every support is welcome, but people who can bear the costs themselves should not receive support. Furthermore, Leefbaar Almelo believes that any help with sustainability measures and alternative energy supplies is desirable for everyone who needs it.

108650.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo finds that too large an impact on our green environment is undesirable. Also, the newly created employment is rarely sufficient and rarely suitable for Almelo.

108651.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes that Almelo must do its best to optimally help and/or shelter everyone.

108655.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo prefers a light show over a fireworks display.

108656.explanation: Although free may be impossible because it will be compensated in waste collection fees and such. Leefbaar Almelo thinks many problems can be solved if no separate fee is charged for takeout.

108657.explanation: According to Leefbaar Almelo, the standards are strict enough.

108658.explanation: For Leefbaar Almelo, such costs are logical business costs. Possibly, there are tax options for investments instead of municipal support. However, it must be mandatory for entrepreneurs to have their business accessible.

108659.explanation: According to Leefbaar Almelo, it is even a legal obligation to channel money to the local broadcaster, although it will soon become a regional broadcaster.

108661.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo wants music lessons, but also sports lessons and other lessons to be possible for everyone, so those who cannot afford it must still be enabled to do so.

108664.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo prefers a different and better focus on prevention in the area of issues that can lead to safety concerns.

108665.explanation: Here too, Leefbaar Almelo hopes for a different and better deployment of people and resources.

108667.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo thinks that other qualities and means may be possible to act effectively in certain cases.

108672.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes that everyone should be able to find suitable housing, so extra housing must also come for students, and according to Leefbaar Almelo, good 'temporary' housing can also be good for the longer term.

108673.explanation: For Leefbaar Almelo, there is a right to benefits. Socially meaningful work is important, but that can be better stimulated and rewarded than mandated as a kind of punishment.

108674.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo thinks this is very harmful and that it does not improve anything but rather worsens things for many people, and ultimately costs more than it supposedly saves.

108675.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo realizes that more and more people are struggling and therefore it seems logical to raise the assistance threshold because even people with a higher income can get into trouble (e.g., 130%).

108677.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo hopes that electric transport increases because it is better for the environment, and then it is logical to realize more charging points. But this must not be at the expense of disabled parking spaces.

108679.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes that the entire (inner) city should be an environmental zone to counter pollution as much as possible.

108680.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo appreciates these options for everyone who wants them, but actually, we would prefer to do away with all these differences.

108681.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo considers the quality of assistance more important than the price, especially since the effectiveness of the best quality help is higher than that of the cheapest help.

108682.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo does not think the municipality should provide this itself, but that institutions/organizations offering these provisions should be well facilitated by the right conditions and circumstances.

108683.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes the property tax (OZB) may be increased, but also that the exemption scheme can be expanded so that hopefully a better distribution according to ability to pay and burden is achieved.

108690.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo considers culture an important facility and therefore Hof88 must receive optimal maintenance and a renewal/improvement effort.

108692.explanation: For those who cannot afford it themselves, the municipality should make this possible, says Leefbaar Almelo.

108693.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes that is a free personal choice for the schools.

108696.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo supports high-rise buildings, among other reasons to make optimal use of scarce space for greenery and water.

108697.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo thinks that in these times of housing shortage, room rentals can provide a solution. There must then be clear criteria and sound contracts protecting everyone's rights.

108698.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo sees a district facility per district as a primary necessity. And where possible, neighborhood centers per neighborhood would be a good addition, possibly managed by residents themselves to increase involvement and responsibility.

108699.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo believes on-street parking should be limited to a minimum and can be made more expensive. Indoor parking and parking garages can possibly be as affordable as possible, although we want to reduce car use.

108701.explanation: This has been Leefbaar Almelo’s aim for years... for various reasons... environment, play space for children, space for greenery and water, sports and playground equipment, seating areas.

108702.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo hopes Almelo will enroll for many more experiments/tests/trials and that in cooperation with all coffeeshops, good and reliable cultivation will be achieved.

108704.explanation: Leefbaar Almelo holds that Almelo should make an optimal contribution to solving the housing shortage, especially when affordability is taken into account.

108644.explanation: Quality and knowledge take precedence over gender.

108645.explanation: The residents of Almelo should have the opportunity to participate in important choices or developments through a referendum.

108648.explanation: The government should determine opening hours in good consultation with hospitality businesses and residents, in the interest of safety.

108649.explanation: The government must encourage residents in terms of sustainability and provide financial support where possible.

108650.explanation: Distribution centers provide employment. The municipality of Almelo has already set a maximum for this development.

108651.explanation: Temporary shelter must be offered acceptably. The municipality should do no more than necessary. Furthermore, shelter locations should not be realized in residential neighborhoods.

108655.explanation: This can be organized by the municipality or by designating locations where associations may organize this under the leadership of the Safety Region Twente (VRT).

108656.explanation: The number of kilos allowed for free disposal at Twente Milieu should be increased to 1000 kg per household.

108657.explanation: Noise standards meet appropriate conditions, and more rules lead to fewer festivals and parties.

108658.explanation: Entrepreneurs must maintain their own properties and also make them accessible to people with disabilities.

108659.explanation: The municipality of Almelo has already made a choice here: one regional broadcaster.

108661.explanation: Music is good for the development of children in primary education. The municipality should support this development.

108664.explanation: Safety, as well as the feeling of safety, can be created by deploying more BOA officers. It is important to grant BOAs more authority.

108665.explanation: Research shows that camera surveillance works, reduces nuisance, and increases the feeling of safety. It is also important to store camera footage and be able to intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

108667.explanation: Provided they are also allowed to use them, otherwise it is pointless. A tougher approach is needed.

108672.explanation: The municipality should focus on property owners to encourage them to convert vacant buildings into student housing.

108673.explanation: Recipients of social assistance benefits should regain regularity by engaging in activities. This will make the step towards work much easier.

108674.explanation: For some residents, the exemption from municipal taxes is a relief, giving them more financial space. That does not prevent the municipality from annually monitoring whether the right groups make use of it.

108675.explanation: The municipality must do its utmost to get residents out of poverty, not just by providing financial support.

108677.explanation: The number of electric cars is growing. Therefore, the municipality must invest in more charging points. This also makes the city more attractive for visitors.

108679.explanation: Delivery to businesses and homes must remain possible. Setting up an environmental zone will bring unnecessary costs for entrepreneurs and residents.

108680.explanation: Care providers must be able to help everyone, regardless of their religion, culture, or beliefs. Doing this increases costs.

108681.explanation: Price in Youth Care is a neglected issue. When it comes to Youth Care, price suddenly seems unimportant. The government must look at content/quality but also sharply at price. If we don’t do this, costs will further get out of control.

108682.explanation: This is not the government’s responsibility. Everyone must take their own responsibility here. The municipality is not Santa Claus.

108683.explanation: Property tax (OZB) must not become a cash cow.

108690.explanation: The building at Hof 88 no longer meets current sustainability standards and needs renovation. The current location is a good spot for this small theater.

108692.explanation: Every child in primary education in Almelo must leave school with a swimming diploma.

108693.explanation: Let primary schools focus on the development of the child. Speaking or understanding Twents will happen naturally.

108696.explanation: At the Station and in the city center, attractive residential towers are being developed. This creates more homes for young and old.

108697.explanation: Renting out individual rooms creates nuisance and unsafe situations.

108699.explanation: Not only cheaper, but parking should be free on weekends and shopping evenings.

108701.explanation: To keep the center attractive, you must be able to park your car in the center. There should be enough parking spaces for this. Good parking garages are a must.

108693.explanation: Preservation of regional heritage and culture is important to Lijst Cete.
