108644.explanation: The administration of the municipality of Almelo must be a reflection of the city’s population. DENK wants diversity quotas and inclusion in the municipal organization—this starts with the executive board.

108645.explanation: DENK Almelo wants democratic, participatory, and inclusive decision-making and strengthening of citizen contributions in local democracy (neighborhood councils, citizen panels) with equal participation; referenda are suitable for decisions with major impact on Almelo (e.g. large-scale construction/central plan).

108648.explanation: Almelo wants liveliness and safety in the city. DENK supports expansion provided there is a points system and clear safety requirements (to limit nuisance in the center/market square).

108649.explanation: In Almelo neighborhoods with high housing costs, joint generation (solar panels/cooperatives) helps reduce energy bills; DENK advocates maintaining the net metering scheme; additional subsidies for insulation and solar panels.

108650.explanation: Almelo has a surplus of jobs per 1,000 residents; expansion may increase housing pressure. DENK wants economic growth linked to housing construction/center vitality; large “big box” centers causing depopulation are rejected.

108651.explanation: DENK Almelo chooses small-scale, humane shelter with quick language/work integration; closure increases pressure elsewhere and harms solidarity within Twente.

108655.explanation: A central fireworks display reduces private nuisance, injuries, and damage to buildings, cars, and goods, and connects Almelo neighborhoods during New Year's Eve.

108656.explanation: Accessible waste disposal prevents illegal dumping in Almelo neighborhoods; sparing low-income households fits with poverty policy.

108657.explanation: Almelo wants a vibrant center and residential quality at the same time. Flexible standards (time/location) with enforceable frameworks are better than generic tightening.

108658.explanation: Accessible shops and care facilities are crucial for Almelo's elderly and residents with disabilities; support speeds up adaptations.

108659.explanation: Neutral, independent local media are necessary for reliable information about Almelo decisions and neighborhoods.

108661.explanation: Access to culture increases opportunities; DENK provides free/subsidized offerings via city passes/minimal income schemes.

108664.explanation: Extra visible enforcement in Almelo’s vulnerable neighborhoods; DENK: community officers without batons but with training/bodycams.

108665.explanation: In Almelo, temporary reinforcement at hotspots (station area, nightlife district); privacy strictly protected, scaling down as soon as no longer needed.

108667.explanation: In Almelo, the focus is on respectful treatment, prevention, and bodycams; preventing escalation through training instead of force.

108672.explanation: With ROC/HBO internships and regional flow, temporary provision (containers/modular) in Almelo is desirable where pressure increases.

108673.explanation: In Almelo: dignified social assistance; appropriate contribution (voluntary/customized) that offers perspective, without distrust. DENK finds it reasonable that residents contribute within their means via an appropriate counterperformance.

108674.explanation: Almelo must spare low-income residents; waiver of fees (waste/sewage) prevents debts and contributes to livelihood security.

108675.explanation: In Almelo, flexible workers/self-employed and low middle incomes are vulnerable; expansion of minimum income schemes (school, sports, care) is necessary.

108677.explanation: Almelo promotes electric driving and shared transport; more charging points in neighborhoods and near facilities, without increasing parking pressure.

108679.explanation: Environmental zones disproportionately affect Almelo's low-income residents; DENK opts for promoting clean mobility without extra barriers.

108680.explanation: Almelo will establish a culturally sensitive care center; tendering requires culturally sensitive care and freedom of choice. Furthermore, DENK advocates mandatory training for care providers in language, communication, and cultural knowledge.

108681.explanation: Almelo must invest in quality and prevention; focus on causes, reducing waiting times. Person-centered care remains the basic standard. 13. Limiting market forces in care; better municipal control to safeguard quality.

108682.explanation: Poverty in Almelo calls for practical relief; making resources available through city passes/neighborhood centers prevents school dropout and enables low-income people to better participate in society.

108683.explanation: DENK wants to cap and keep property tax (OZB) fair (not milk the cow), but a general reduction could affect facilities (care/education); customized measures are needed in case of explosive property value increases.

108690.explanation: DENK Almelo wants a revision of the theater plan (500 seats too small); first demonstrate that renewal is better than larger or no new theater—using Almelo’s needs as the benchmark.

108692.explanation: DENK Almelo: restore school swimming levels A+B. Fits with health/safety and equal opportunities in Almelo neighborhoods.

108693.explanation: Multilingualism and local culture contribute to inclusion; schools retain space for their own approach in Almelo.

108696.explanation: High-rise near public transport hubs (station/center) can alleviate housing shortage in Almelo without additional land use. (Location bound).

108697.explanation: Almelo must firmly combat abuses (overcrowding/exploitation), but allow quality rooming with permits/enforcement for students/single persons. Strict conditions can be set for sufficient living and housing space.

108698.explanation: Neighborhood centers are crucial for Almelo: meeting, assistance, digital inclusion, sports/culture (social cohesion).

108699.explanation: Almelo wants an accessible inner city; lower/ cap tariffs to counteract shop vacancies.

108701.explanation: Car-restricted zones are okay, but Almelo must remain accessible for care, shopping, and entrepreneurs; maintain parking capacity. Also, it is not good for people with mobility impairments.

108702.explanation: Almelo decides only after livability/safety assessment and strong prevention/addiction care; no increase of nuisance in vulnerable neighborhoods.

108704.explanation: Almelo has a housing shortage; DENK wants to accelerate (cut procedures, increase planning capacity) and build affordably (45/40/15) instead of reducing numbers.

108644.explanation: We believe that the College of Mayor and Aldermen should be a good reflection of society.

108645.explanation: It is important to bring more democracy into decisions, but the referendum is not suitable for this. Complex issues are turned into a yes-no question. Volt prefers new initiatives such as a citizens' parliament where residents give binding advice to the municipal council.

108648.explanation: Volt values a lively city center, but nuisance must be limited.

108649.explanation: It is good that the government rewards sustainability. Think of subsidies for generating and/or storing energy. But the government should also help residents with insulation, so less energy is consumed.

108650.explanation: Volt wants more emphasis on high-tech and creative professions. Distribution and transshipment are already sufficiently present in Almelo. The question whether new industrial sites are needed depends strongly for Volt on the question 'What kind of companies?'.

108651.explanation: Almelo has contributed significantly for some time to refugee reception and supporting their integration. Our city shows a warm heart in this. Volt fully supports this policy.

108655.explanation: Volt wants an annual central fireworks show instead of private fireworks, to prevent air pollution, animal suffering, and injuries. A collective fireworks show is a great occasion to come together and professionally handle the fireworks.

108656.explanation: Taking bulky waste to the dump is difficult for many people. This can lead to illegal dumping. Being able to have bulky waste collected once or twice a year for free can reduce littering. The costs are distributed among everyone.

108657.explanation: Loud music at festivals can cause an annoying ringing in the ears that never goes away (tinnitus) and lifelong hearing damage. Volt wants to protect visitors from this. Also, softer music can create an equally good festival experience as louder music.

108658.explanation: Volt wants accessibility for everyone. This includes accessibility of buildings. Volt finds it important that work is done on this and wants the municipality to promote it.

108659.explanation: Volt wants to examine whether the current agreement with 1Twente is effective, both substantively and in terms of costs. If not, Volt supports restoring a local broadcaster supported by volunteers.

108661.explanation: Volt considers cultural education very important. Art, dance, and music provide relaxation and bring people together. Volt wants every child to receive lessons in these subjects at school. Music lessons through the music school should be accessible to everyone, and subsidized by the municipality where necessary.

108664.explanation: Volt finds safety very important. Almelo has a shortage of enforcement officers (BOAs), including for the outskirts (green BOAs) and city center supervisors. Therefore, more BOAs need to be appointed.

108665.explanation: Everyone should feel safe in public spaces. Volt wants to identify unsafe areas and actively address them. This can be done, for example, by making these places more attractive, ensuring sufficient lighting and visibility, and possibly installing cameras.

108667.explanation: BOAs’ equipment should match the tasks they perform. BOAs do not actively engage in dangerous situations and should leave such situations to the police. Volt believes carrying weapons like batons is unnecessary as it provokes aggression and increases the likelihood of use.

108672.explanation: Volt supports appropriate housing types for every category of residents. Almelo is not a specific student city, so special policies for this group seem unnecessary. Volt also prefers permanent solutions over temporary ones.

108673.explanation: People do not end up on welfare benefits lightly. Mandatory work causes extra stress and is often not beneficial in the long term. The municipality can encourage (volunteer) work, however.

108674.explanation: Volt is pragmatic. Bills must be paid, but sometimes it's better for the long term to forgive them. The municipality should make a careful case-by-case assessment with a 'human scale,' considering income and the additional stress caused by the debt.

108675.explanation: Volt wants to reduce poverty, but it must also be financially feasible. It is better to assess the support needed on a case-by-case basis. Even more important is that the municipality helps people navigate the various support measures.

108677.explanation: Driving electric is an important part of the energy transition, but not yet accessible to everyone. Therefore, more public charging points must be established, well distributed across neighborhoods, so there are enough facilities for people who cannot charge their car at home.

108679.explanation: Where other European municipalities have successfully implemented this measure, Almelo should also investigate this possibility.

108680.explanation: Due to aging, there are major shortages in healthcare making this unfeasible. The municipality does not determine healthcare organizations’ policies and should not want to. The municipal healthcare obligation includes providing support that matches someone's care needs, not their beliefs.

108681.explanation: The municipality must make a good balance between price and quality. Overpriced care should be rejected, as should care that does not meet quality standards.

108682.explanation: One in eight women in the Netherlands experiences period poverty, meaning they do not have enough money to access menstrual products properly. The municipality can do a lot for them by supporting and supplementing existing initiatives.

108683.explanation: Lowering the property tax (OZB) increases inequality because it significantly reduces municipal income, forcing cuts in many areas. This would be a benefit for homeowners, who often already have lower fixed costs, at the expense of residents in greater need.

108690.explanation: New theater: Small-scale, powerful, and centrally located. Not a new large theater with huge costs, but a small theater like Hof 88, with 300 to 400 seats. This creates room for a broader cultural offering without competing with theaters in Hengelo and Enschede.

108692.explanation: Current facilities are sufficient; this is no extra task for the municipality or schools.

108693.explanation: Culture makes us human and connects us. In Almelo, Twents and the Low Saxon history are important parts of this. The municipality does not decide what is taught but can promote after-school activities or support organizations involved in this.

108696.explanation: Volt wants more housing to be built. This also requires high-rise residential buildings, because they leave more space for greenery.

108697.explanation: Volt wants the housing shortage resolved as quickly as possible. Renting out individual rooms can help. Many people live in large homes and are willing to rent out (part of) their home, for example to students or divorced individuals. Abuse like substandard rental housing must be countered.

108698.explanation: Volt supports neighborhood and district centers because they foster social cohesion and accessible care. In some cases, small neighborhoods can collaborate.

108699.explanation: Choosing lower parking costs means more traffic and crowded streets. Volt opts to invest in accessible public transport and good cycling and walking facilities to and within the city center.

108701.explanation: The city center is attractive without (many) cars. Volt finds it important to make the center as car-free as possible, while considering the residents living there.

108702.explanation: Volt states that legal cannabis cultivation in Almelo can break the link to crime and improve public health. It ends illogical tolerance, increases street safety, and prevents fire hazards in residential areas by moving cultivation to secure, professional locations.

108704.explanation: Housing is a basic right. Yet many residents face barriers due to the lack of homes — especially suitable homes. Volt wants to solve this shortage in a social, sustainable, and future-oriented way.

The BOAs who monitor safety in Almelo should be issued batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Almelo controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to Twents.
Almelo’s center should become car-free.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Almelo should allow more high-rise residential buildings.
Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
Almelo should renovate Theater Hof 88.
Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The planned number of 14,000 new homes should be reduced.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.
The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
